title: ISE Identity Risk Score Contribution to Splunk ES Risk-Based Alerting (RBA)
dataSources: Splunk ES `risk_index` populated via the `Splunk_TA_cisco_identity_services` add-on's risk modular outputs (or via a custom risk-correlation rule). Risk modifiers from ISE include: failed authentications (5400), posture non-compliance (80002), TC-NAC threat (86040), MDM compliance failure (86200), CII risk score >70 (86001 with risk reason).
app: Splunk Enterprise Security + `Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services`.
spl:
| from datamodel:"Risk"."All_Risk"
| where source IN ("ISE-AuthFail", "ISE-PostureFail", "ISE-TCNAC", "ISE-CII")
| stats sum(risk_score) as total_ise_risk by risk_object
| where total_ise_risk > 80
description: Tracks how ISE-derived risk events contribute to Splunk ES RBA risk-object scores. Identifies users or endpoints whose ISE-only risk contribution exceeds a threshold — useful both for tuning RBA rules and for surfacing identity-anchored threats.
value: RBA aggregates many low-confidence signals into a high-confidence alert. Tracking ISE's contribution helps understand which ISE signals are pulling risk-objects above the alert threshold and which are background noise.
implementation: Schedule every 30 min. Requires ES Risk framework configured.
visualization: ISE-risk contribution by source; top risk objects from ISE-only events.
knownFalsePositives:
(a) Test accounts intentionally trigger many fails.
(b) Posture-grace-period users.
mitreAttack: N/A (ES integration — measures Risk-Based Alerting score-to-action effectiveness, no adversary technique)

compliance.assurance_rationale: RA-3 risk assessment — continuous risk scoring.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Trigger ISE-side: 10 failed auths + posture fail. ES Risk DM aggregates; UC-17.1.81 surfaces user.
controlTest.negativeScenario: No ISE risk activity. No fire.

grandmaExplanation: Splunk ES gives every user and device a risk score that goes up when bad things happen. We measure how much ISE events contribute to those risk scores — to see if ISE is helping or just adding noise.
